search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk NG127 foot numbness sensory symptoms adults weakness gait referral
site:nice.org.uk NG19 diabetic foot sensory examination perfusion ulcer
candidate_sources_rejected: diabetic-foot guidance applied when diabetes is not established
automatic blood tests, imaging, medicine, podiatry, neurology, or vascular referral
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Numbness has neurological, metabolic, spinal, vascular, compressive, medicine, and local causes.
The diabetic-foot source applies only in confirmed diabetes context.
No test, treatment, or referral is generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults with foot numbness; children, pregnancy, acute stroke, cauda equina, trauma, diabetes, vascular disease, infection, ulceration, and rapid progression require tailored pathways.
setting_applicability: Primary-care documentation and follow-up as qualified by the exact source and clinical context.
UAE_applicability: International evidence requires UAE primary-care, laboratory, prescribing, referral, safeguarding, and local-pathway adaptation.
recency_verification: Exact official documents and sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: The cited exact source remains current on the issuing organisation website at the review date.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact adult sensory-symptom section supports onset, distribution, unilateral or symmetrical pattern, progression, weakness, imbalance, gait, reflex, back or radicular, bowel or bladder, metabolic-test, and referral context without assigning neuropathy. The exact neurological section supports recording existing metabolic, neurological, or imaging results and clinician-entered follow-up or referral status without generating tests.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports sudden unilateral numbness, rapidly progressive symmetrical sensory change with weakness or imbalance, recurrent sensory episodes, distal sensory distribution, reflex and gait context, selected metabolic tests, radicular symptoms, bowel or bladder context, and clinician-led referral.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact diabetic-foot section supports clinician-performed bilateral skin, sensory, perfusion, ulcer, callus, infection, deformity, gangrene, and Charcot assessment only when diabetes and examination context apply.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting age-appropriate review, bilateral skin and sensory examination, perfusion, ulceration, callus, infection or inflammation, deformity, gangrene, Charcot features, and clinician-entered risk classification without calculating it.

unresolved_source_gaps: Laterality, distribution, onset, weakness, gait, diabetes, vascular status, spine symptoms, examination, cause, diagnosis, test, treatment, referral, and urgency remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.

All previously supported GP legacy-item mappings were conservatively removed because exact item identity, direct support, and item-specific applicability were not independently established under the fail-closed contract.
